summary: The "dead earClip/efg path" hypothesis is CONFIRMED with one important correction and one safety caveat. (1) Dead in production: leaf.efg is typed (PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:65) but NEVER assigned anywhere in src/ except test fixtures â€” both leaves() (PBQ:555-565) and leafOfCell() (PBQ:584-593) construct leaves without efg, and shapedTemplate() early-returns false when efg is absent (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:101), so earClipMaxMinAngle and the shorter-3D-diagonal plain-quad split are unreachable regardless of uBias. (2) The exact gate is: leaf.efg present AND (uBias>0 OR cellAspect3D(efg)>âˆš3(1+1e-6)) â€” efg presence is the master switch. (3) Mechanically the ear-clip is strictly interior (emits only existing polygon indices, never calls vertexIndex), but the absolute watertight-safety claim has an unproven corner: the final-3 emission (QT:188-190) is not area-guarded and the greedy loop can break (QT:181) on an all-collinear remainder â€” both reachable in principle because these polygons are only WEAKLY convex (corner+mid+corner are 3 collinear points). (4) Populating efg is cheap and small-surface: retain the sampler as a field and compute firstFundamentalForm (SurfaceMetricTensor.ts:115-126, 4 bilinear position() samples) at each leaf centre inside leaves(); periodic-u is already handled inside GpuSurfaceSampler.position (SurfaceSampler.ts:88-94) and t-clamping inside clampStepT. (5) Re-baseline scope: every wall triangulated via triangulateQuadtree changes wherever the gate fires â€” i.e., ALL leaves on uBias>0 walls (the GATE-B styles, including INNER walls of CDT styles since both walls share uBias), and only >~2:1-aspect cells on B=0 walls. The CDT path (triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures) bypasses the shaped templates entirely â€” its plain-cell template is hardcoded legacy (FCT:687-707) and never reads efg â€” so step A cannot fix SFB@1/Voronoi outer-wall defects. (6) Hidden coupling correction to the handoff: it is not just the helix warp â€” the u-warp (PEC:2418-2422) and t-warp (PEC:2434-2441) are ALSO applied to vertices post-triangulation, so a plain-sampler efg is metrically wrong on any warp-pinned style (Gothic, GeometricStar, BambooSegments...), not only SpiralRidges; warp composition should cover all three. Also, the shaped path changes vertex COUNT (drops the legacy centroid on every transition cell), not just connectivity.

verifiedClaims.claim: earClipMaxMinAngle/shapedTemplate is truly unreachable in production because leaf.efg is never populated
verifiedClaims.evidence: Grep over potfoundry-web/src finds efg assigned ONLY in QuadtreeTriangulator.test.ts fixtures (lines 333-472). PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.leaves() (PBQ:555-565) maps cells to {u0,t0,level,iu,it,uExtra} â€” no efg; leafOfCell() (PBQ:584-593, used only by neighbors() at :577) likewise omits it. QuadtreeTriangulator reads leaf.efg at QT:339 and computes aniso=shapedTemplate(efg,...) at QT:345; shapedTemplate returns false immediately when !efg (QT:101), and all three shaped branches additionally require `aniso && efg` (QT:464, 499, 525). The only production QuadtreeLike fed to triangulateQuadtree is a PeriodicBalancedQuadtree (ConformingWall.ts:209, 446-447).

verifiedClaims.claim: The path is gated by uBias>0 or an aniso flag
verifiedClaims.evidence: The MASTER gate is efg presence: shapedTemplate (QT:100-104) is `if (!efg) return false; if (uBias > 0) return true; return cellAspect3D(efg,du,dt) > ANISO_ASPECT_GATE`. So uBias>0 alone does NOT activate it (today every GATE-B style with B>0 still gets legacy templates because efg is absent). Once efg is populated: B>0 â‡’ shaped path on EVERY leaf; B=0 â‡’ only leaves whose F-inclusive 3D aspect exceeds âˆš3(1+1e-6) (QT:71).

verifiedClaims.claim: The QT:63-71 comment that an isotropic cell scores âˆš3 so the gate sits 'a hair above' isotropy
verifiedClaims.evidence: cellAspect3D (QT:84-91) uses longest2 = max(EÂ·duÂ², GÂ·dtÂ²) â€” the larger axis LEG, not the hypotenuse â€” over 2Â·(quad area). For E=G, F=0, du=dt this yields âˆš3/2, not âˆš3; the gate âˆš3(1+1e-6) is therefore crossed at a physical aspect ratio of ~2:1 (Ï>2 for F=0), not just above 1:1. The comment matches a hypotenuse-inclusive variant that is not what the code computes. Functional effect at B=0: mildly anisotropic cells (aspect 1â€“2) keep legacy templates â€” conservative, and the 'isotropic falls through byte-for-byte' claim still holds.

verifiedClaims.claim: earClip is strictly INTERIOR â€” polygon/shared-edge vertices unchanged â€” and therefore watertight-safe (QT:142-147)
verifiedClaims.evidence: Interior-only: CONFIRMED â€” earClipMaxMinAngle (QT:149-191) emits exclusively via emit(idx[ip],idx[ic],idx[inx]) (QT:185,189) over the caller-supplied boundary indices; it never calls vertexIndex() and never adds/moves/subdivides a vertex, unlike the legacy centre-fan which inserts vertexIndex(um,tm) (QT:502-503, 528-529). The boundary polygon itself (corners + registry-union mids, QT:482-531) is identical for both templates, so shared-edge vertex sets are unchanged. Watertight-safety caveat: REFUTED as an absolute. The polygons are weakly convex (on-edge mids are collinear with their corners). Two unguarded corners exist: (a) the final-3 emission (QT:188-190) has no area check â€” if the greedy strands 3 collinear points (e.g. SW,mid,SE) it emits a zero-area triangle (topologically manifold but a geometric sliver); (b) if >3 all-collinear vertices remain, no positive-area ear exists, the loop breaks (QT:181) and those boundary sub-edges get NO triangle in this cell â€” a one-sided edge, i.e. a real crack. Reachability requires the max-min-angle greedy to remove the last off-line vertex early (plausible: the apex ear is the big well-shaped triangle); 'termination in exactly kâˆ’2 triangles' (QT:146-147) is only guaranteed for strictly convex input. Needs a brute-force/property test before trusting the claim.

verifiedClaims.claim: firstFundamentalForm exists at PBQ ~:811

verifiedClaims.claim: The CDT path (FeatureConformingTriangulator) bypasses the shaped templates entirely
verifiedClaims.evidence: Routing: ConformingWall.buildWallMeshAtScale returns triangulateQuadtree(qt) only when clippedFeatures.length===0 (ConformingWall.ts:447); otherwise triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures (ConformingWall.ts:464). So populating efg changes NOTHING on feature-bearing outer walls (SFB, Voronoi, HexagonalHive, CelticKnot/Triquetra, BasketWeave braids...); their slivers/nonMan need the separate CDT fix (handoff step C). verifiedClaims.claim: For SpiralRidges the efg must be warp-composed because the helix shear is applied after triangulation
verifiedClaims.evidence: applyHelixWarp runs over assembled vertices AFTER triangulation (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2466-2481; wall vertices use their own t as shear parameter at :2470-2471). The quadtree/sampler operate in PRE-warp (u,t), so plain-sampler efg describes the unsheared surface; the final mesh's map is u_final=Ï†â‚€(u)âˆ’(turns/k)Â·t, whose pullback metric gains exactly the F cross-term that makes SpiralRidges cells parallelograms. ADDITIONAL finding the handoff misses: the vertical-crease u-warp (PEC:2418-2422) and horizontal t-warp (PEC:2434-2441, walls only) are ALSO post-triangulation vertex remaps â€” a non-identity Ï† stretches E by Ï†'Â² locally â€” so plain efg is metrically wrong near pinned creases on Gothic/GeometricStar/BambooSegments-class styles too, not only SpiralRidges. Step B should compose whichever warp is active (helix XOR u-warp, plus t-warp), not just the helix.

verifiedClaims.claim: shapedTemplate changes only connectivity, not vertex count
verifiedClaims.evidence: On transition cells (single-mid QT:482-504; N-mid QT:508-530) the legacy template INSERTS an interior centroid vertex via vertexIndex(um,tm) (QT:502, 528) and emits k triangles (k = polygon size); the ear-clip inserts NO vertex and emits kâˆ’2 triangles. So activating the path reduces both vertex and triangle counts on every shaped transition cell â€” any count-based baselines/snapshots will shift. Plain quads (QT:453-479) keep the same 4 vertices/2 triangles; only the diagonal may flip (shorter 3D diagonal, with QSCALE tie-quantization at QT:468-470 resolving exact ties to legacy SWâ†’NE). Orientation is preserved: ears require signedArea2 > 1e-18 i.e. CCW (QT:173-174), the legacy fan is CCW, and seam handling is template-independent (per-leaf wrapsSeam flag QT:343/421, unwrapped u coords during triangulation, u=1â†’u=0 remap at QT:533-548). 2:1-balance/transition machinery is untouched: both templates consume the identical registry-built boundary polygon.

mechanics: earClipMaxMinAngle (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:149-191): given the leaf's CCW boundary polygon (4 corners + on-edge mids from the PASS-A/B grid-line registry) and the leaf's constant first fundamental form efg={E,F,G}, it repeatedly scans every candidate ear (prev,cur,next) over the remaining vertex ring, rejects non-CCW/collinear ears via 2D signed area <= 1e-18 (QT:173-174), scores each valid ear by its minimum interior 3D angle computed by law-of-cosines on metric lengths |PuÂ·du+PtÂ·dt|Â² = EÂ·duÂ²+2FÂ·duÂ·dt+GÂ·dtÂ² (metricLen2 QT:79-81, triMinAngle3D QT:107-127), clips the best-scoring ear (locally max-min-angle), and emits the final 3 unconditionally. It is O(kÂ²) per polygon (k small: 4-8 typically), uses only existing global vertex indices (no Steiner points, no centroid), and the metric is the leaf-centre efg treated as constant over the cell â€” i.e., a per-cell anisotropic-quality diagonal/ear selector. The plain-quad variant (QT:459-479) just compares the two diagonals' metric lengths and picks the shorter in 3D, with quantized tie â†’ legacy SWâ†’NE. Activation per leaf is precomputed as aniso=shapedTemplate(efg, sizeU, sizeT, uBias) (QT:345): efg present AND (B>0 OR F-inclusive 3D aspect > âˆš3(1+1e-6), which the code formula reaches at ~2:1 physical aspect).
fixSurface: Step A (populate efg) is a small, contained change in PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts only: (1) retain the constructor's `metric: SurfaceSampler` as a private readonly field (it is currently used in refine/localDirectionalRefine and discarded â€” fields list PBQ:101-175 has no metric); (2) in leaves() (PBQ:555-565) and leafOfCell() (PBQ:584-593), add efg: firstFundamentalForm(this.metric, (iu+0.5)/2^eUL, (it+0.5)/2^level, this.steps.hu, this.steps.ht). No public signature changes anywhere â€” QuadLeaf.efg already exists (PBQ:65) and QuadtreeLike already passes it through (QT:24-33, 339). Periodic-u needs NOTHING: GpuSurfaceSampler.position wraps u internally (SurfaceSampler.ts:88-94) and firstFundamentalForm just calls position(uÂ±hu,t); t is clamped by clampStepT (SurfaceMetricTensor.ts:98-102). Perf: 4 bilinear CPU samples per leaf, once per final build â€” the same FFF is already evaluated per visited cell during refinement (PBQ:332), and the budget binary-search calls only leafCount() (ConformingWall.ts:246-247), never leaves(), so lazily computing in leaves() adds zero cost to the search; expect well under 1% of the 7-36s build. Do NOT tag at addLeaf time â€” cells are reconstructed from string keys (cellOfKey PBQ:277-284) with no payload slot, and balance()/directionalUSplit() create leaves after refinement; a parallel Map would be more invasive. Step B (warp-composed efg) must transform the sampled {E,F,G} by the pullback of the post-triangulation remap: helix u_final=Ï†â‚€(u)âˆ’sÂ·t gives E'=EÂ·Ï†â‚€'Â², F'=Ï†â‚€'(Fâˆ’sE), G'=Gâˆ’2sF+sÂ²E; the plain u-warp/t-warp need the analogous Ï†'Â²/Ïˆ'Â² scalings â€” but the warps are chosen in ParametricExportComputer (PEC:2315, 2354-2360) AFTER the samplers exist, so threading them into the quadtree requires passing a metric-transform hook down through assembleWatertight â†’ buildConformingWall â†’ buildQuadtreeAtScale, the only structural change of the plan. Step C (CDT non-manifold/anisotropic fill) is entirely separate: FeatureConformingTriangulator never consults efg and would need its own metric-aware fill.
risks: Re-baseline blast radius is large once efg is populated: on every wall whose uBias B>0 (GATE-B styles per WatertightAssembly.computeUBias:110-152 â€” the 9-style slivered set, plus SFB@high) shapedTemplate fires on ALL leaves, including the INNER walls of CDT styles (inner is always feature-free and shares the outer's uBias, WatertightAssembly.ts:417,429). The project's hard constraint (clean-style default output byte-identical unless explicitly re-baselined with full re-measure) WILL be violated for those styles â€” plan an explicit re-baseline; B=0 smooth styles should stay byte-identical only if all their cells sit under the ~2:1 aspect gate (verify per style, don't assume).

earClipMaxMinAngle's watertight guarantee has two unguarded corners on weakly-convex polygons (collinear on-edge mids): an area-unchecked final-3 emission (QT:188-190) can emit a zero-area triangle, and a no-valid-ear break (QT:181) on an all-collinear remainder (>3 vertices) would leave boundary sub-edges with no incident triangle in the cell â€” a real crack. Reachability depends on the greedy order (the well-shaped 'apex' ear can outscore the thin end ears); write a brute-force property test over rectangle+mids polygons under anisotropic metrics BEFORE shipping, or add a guard (prefer ears whose removal keeps a non-collinear remainder / area-check the final 3 and re-split).

The handoff's step B is under-scoped: not just the helix warp but ALSO the vertical-crease u-warp (PEC:2418-2422) and horizontal t-warp (PEC:2434-2441) are applied to vertices post-triangulation, so plain-sampler efg is wrong near pinned creases on ANY warp-pinned style (Gothic, GeometricStar, BambooSegments, RidgedFlute-class). If step A ships without composing these, the ear-clip will optimize the WRONG metric exactly in the crest neighbourhoods the fix targets â€” potentially worsening serration locally while improving bulk.

Step A does nothing for the CDT styles' outer walls (SFB@1 non-manifold at auto-B3, Voronoi nonMan=1, level-set curve-needle slivers): triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures never reads efg and its plain-cell template is hardcoded legacy (FCT:687-707). Do not expect those metrics to move; they need step C in FeatureConformingTriangulator (suspect dedupSide/weld region, FCT:713-749+).

Vertex/triangle counts DROP on every shaped transition cell (centroid omitted, kâ†’kâˆ’2 triangles): any snapshot, count-based test, LOD heuristic, or budget calibration (TRIS_PER_LEAF=2, ConformingWall.ts:181 â€” fan cells currently emit MORE than 2 tris/leaf) will shift slightly; re-check the budget search's accuracy assumptions after activation.

The ANISO_ASPECT_GATE comment/code mismatch (QT:63-71 vs 84-91: isotropic scores âˆš3/2 in code, so the gate â‰ˆ2:1 aspect, not 'a hair above isotropic') means B=0 cells with aspect 1-2 keep legacy templates; if measurements after step A show residual slivers on B=0 styles, check whether the intended gate was the hypotenuse-inclusive variant before lowering the threshold.

The per-leaf efg is the leaf-CENTRE metric treated as constant; on feature-dense styles with sub-cell relief (the exact crest cells being targeted) the centre sample can misrepresent the edge regions â€” same class of curved-surface error that killed refineCellInterior. The ear-clip only chooses among existing diagonals (it cannot manufacture positions), so the failure mode is 'suboptimal diagonal', not geometric corruption â€” but measure crestBandTriangleQuality before/after rather than assuming the gain.

The three template sites: plain-quad shorter-3D-diagonal (459-479, tie-quantized to legacy), single-mid (482-504) and N-mid (508-530) â€” each branches on `aniso && efg`; legacy branch inserts centroid vertexIndex(um,tm) at 502/528 (vertex-count difference)

CDT path's plain-cell template â€” hardcoded legacy SWâ†’NE diagonal + centre-fan; zero efg references in the whole file (step A cannot affect feature-bearing outer walls)

POST-triangulation vertex remaps: u-warp, t-warp, helix warp â€” all three invalidate a plain-sampler efg in their support; step B must compose the active warp(s), not only the helix

area: Conforming mesher feature-cell fill: potfoundry-web/src/renderers/webgpu/parametric/conforming/{FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts, ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts}, with supporting reads of QuadtreeTriangulator.ts, PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts, ConformingWall.ts, FeatureLineGraph.ts, WatertightAssembly.ts, ParametricExportComputer.ts
summary: Feature-crossed cells are filled by a PER-CELL cdt2d constrained Delaunay (ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:77) run in RAW (u,t) coordinates with zero quality constraints and zero Steiner insertion of its own â€” the only Steiners are braid-crossing splits from planarizeConstraints. Across-the-crest slivers are inherent: the only vertices available are corners/mids/crest-crossings, and (u,t)-Delaunay is blind to the 3D metric, so triangles bridging a diagonal crest chord to the opposite cell edge are 3D needles. The plain-cell branch of the feature path (FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts:687-707) center-fans unconditionally â€” it never calls the metric-aware earClipMaxMinAngle that already exists (dead) in QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:149-191; porting it requires (a) populating leaf.efg in PeriodicBalancedQuadtree (never set in production â€” confirmed) and (b) exporting the currently module-private Efg helpers. The dedupSide/weld region is cross-cell SYMMETRIC by design (registry union + min-qk canonical); the stronger root-cause hypothesis for the SFB@1 B=3 non-manifold is an ORDER-OF-OPERATIONS bug: constraint planarization (PASS A, :634) runs BEFORE the PASS-B endpoint merges (dedupSide :743-760, interior weld :777-791, nearest-fallback resolve :798-813), so merging endpoints of two different dense crest chords under the 8:1-anisotropic snap box (cornerSnapU shrinks 2^-B, cornerSnapT does not â€” :263-264) can re-introduce interior-crossing constraints, which cdt2d cannot handle ("braids self-overlap", :204); the fold-over is then silently masked by the negative-area winding flip (ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:84-86), producing exactly duplicated-edge non-manifoldness. Principled fix = re-planarize after endpoint resolution + treat pre-flip negative area as a per-cell error signal, not capping B. The watertight contract permits an anisotropy-aware interior fill: boundary polygon vertices are sacrosanct (replayed as a closed cdt2d constraint loop), but interior edge flips, interior constraint splits, and interior Steiners â‰¥2e-6 from any side are all legal â€” the refineCellInterior wiring (:836-842) is the existing replay template. Per-cell crest tangents need no new plumbing: each cell's data.constraints already holds the clipped crest chords (bâˆ’a = tangent), and SpiralRidges' slope is closed-form (âˆ’turns/k) but lives in a POST-triangulation vertex shear (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2466-2481), so its efg must be warp-composed.

verifiedClaims.claim: SFB@1 B=3 non-manifold is a quantization/rounding asymmetry in dedupSide under high anisotropy
verifiedClaims.evidence: Static analysis cannot reproduce the 3-duplicated/4-edge count without running the export. Code reading argues AGAINST a cross-cell dedup asymmetry (both cells read the identical registry set :679-682 and min-qk canonical :750 is order-independent; cleanEdge :733-738 is symmetric between same-level neighbours) and FOR an intra-cell constraint-topology corruption: planarize runs before endpoint merging, and ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:84-86 silently flips negative-area (folded) triangles â€” a fold-over of two triangles yields duplicated directed edges, matching the observed signature. mechanics:
Q1 â€” HOW FEATURE CELLS ARE FILLED. Pipeline (FeatureConformingTriangulator.triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures, entry ConformingWall.ts:464-467 with cornerSnap = 0.06/2^featureLevel :451): (1) feature polyline points snapped onto containing-cell edges (snapToCellEdge :307-346); (2) per-cell Liangâ€“Barsky clip of candidate segments â†’ interior chords, plus per-edge-LINE crossings so both neighbours derive bit-identical points (:570-575, clipToBox :108-141, edgeCrossingsInto :153-177); (3) endpoints snapped to corner/mid anchors (snapToAnchor :614-620), registered to a grid-line registry (:586-599, regH/regV :514-522); (4) crossing constraints split at proper intersections (planarizeConstraints :208-241, applied :634-637 â€” the ONLY systematic Steiner source); (5) PASS B builds the cell boundary polygon from corners + transition mids + the registry UNION (:679-682, :761-769) and calls triangulateConstrainedCell (:822-826) = cdt2d({exterior:false}) with the boundary loop itself as constraint edges (ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:73-77). NO quality bound, NO refinement; Delaunay is judged in raw (u,t) (xy = [p.u, p.t] :76), not the 3D metric. WHERE THE ACROSS-CREST SLIVERS COME FROM: a diagonal crest chord crosses an axis-aligned cell; the CDT's only vertices are the 4 corners, â‰¤4 mids, the crest's edge crossings, and crest interior vertices. Triangles connecting the chord to the far corner/edge span the whole cell; under âˆšE/âˆšGâ‰«1 a (u,t)-plausible triangle is a 3D needle, and successive cells along the crest alternate which side the long triangles fall on â†’ sawtooth silhouette. summary:
Q1 â€” How auto-uBias B is computed (WatertightAssembly.ts:110-152). computeUBias(sampler, hasFeatures) first computes a RELIEF-FREE shape ratio on a 16x17 position lattice: wideFlat = 2*pi*meanRadius/zRange (lines 113-128). GATE A (line 133): if wideFlat > UBIAS_AREF*sqrt(2) = 3*1.414 ~ 4.24 (UBIAS_AREF line 38) it returns the FIXED UBIAS_WIDE_B=2 (line 54), or 0 when hasFeatures (deferral kept because B>0 amplifies GAP-2 curve-needle slivers on Voronoi/Gyroid â€” comment lines 129-132, 352-365). GATE B (lines 149-151, the !wideFlat branch, fires at DEFAULT dims): maxURatio = classifySurfaceShear(sampler).maxURatio, the WORST u-dominant sqrt(E)/sqrt(G) over a 192x192 lattice with tMargin 0.02 (FShearDiagnostics.ts:172-176, 205-210); b = round(log2(maxURatio/sqrt(3))) clamped to [0, MAX_RELIEF_B=4] (line 60). Why B=3 on SuperformulaBlossom@1: maxURatio is a single-worst-point MAX, not a median/percentile â€” SFB@1's steep petal flanks hit ~11.8 at isolated lattice points (documented in-code at lines 100 and 143), and round(log2(11.8/1.732)) = round(2.77) = 3. That worst point then sets B for the ENTIRE wall (the quadtree root becomes a 2^B x 1 grid, PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:347-350). GATE B is feature-agnostic by design â€” hasFeatures is consumed only by GATE A (doc lines 106-107) â€” so CDT-insertion styles get the full B with no cap. The non-manifold then materializes downstream in FeatureConformingTriangulator's side-point dedup, whose merge tolerance is bias-dependent (posTolU = cornerSnapU/sizeU, FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts:739-744 â€” 'Equal at B=0'); at B=3 the u-cells are 8x narrower so the relative snap window over a constrained-curve-crossed edge grows, the documented suspect for the 3 duplicated/total=4 edges.

Q2 â€” Where a watertight cap on B for CDT styles would go. The natural seam is computeUBias itself: it already receives hasFeatures (line 110) but ignores it in GATE B; a cap is a one-line change at line 151, e.g. min(b, hasFeatures ? 2 : MAX_RELIEF_B). hasFeatures is true exactly for the CDT-insertion styles: assembleWatertight sets it from opts.outerFeatureLines (WatertightAssembly.ts:366), which the orchestrator populates only with kind==='general-curve' lines (ParametricExportComputer.ts:2329-2331) â€” warp-pinned creases travel separately as outerCreaseLines (refine-only, never CDT). The dev override __pfConformingUBias (lines 371-374) is the existing bisection lever. Justifying measurement: (a) replicate the existing pinning evidence â€” SFB@1 auto B=3 => nonMan=3 (4-dup edges), forced B=2 => topology-clean â€” via the faithful e2e export-fidelity gate (diagnoseTopoQuality on real exports, per the watertight-gate memory; unit tests overstate); (b) a B-sweep {0,1,2,3} over ALL outerFeatureLines styles (Hive, Voronoi, Gyroid, CelticKnot/Triquetra, BasketWeave, SFB@1) at default dims recording bnd/nonMan/orient + sliverCount + crestBandTriangleQuality, showing B<=2 watertight for every CDT style and quantifying the quality cost (SFB@1 keeps a 10.4% sub-15-degree residual at B=2 â€” the cap is a topology guard, not the quality fix); (c) byte-identity check that non-feature walls are untouched (the cap only branches on hasFeatures). Unit-level: extend ComputeUBias.test.ts (it already imports/exports the gates, lines 14, 37-126).

Q3 â€” MetricSizingField is fully ISOTROPIC and scalar. The grid stores ONE target edge length per (u,t) node (Float64Array resU x resT, MetricSizingField.ts:63, 81); it derives from principalCurvatureMax â€” a single max-principal-curvature scalar (line 93; defined SurfaceMetricTensor.ts:144) â€” via the sagitta law h = sqrt(8*maxSag/kappa) (line 100), clamped (line 104), then scalar Lipschitz-graded over 4-neighbours (gradeLipschitz, lines 119-144), and read back by bilinear edgeLength(u,t) (lines 147-172). E/F/G never enter the field. The metric enters only at the consumer: PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.shouldRefine converts the scalar target to per-axis physical extents physW=sqrt(E)*du, physH=sqrt(G)*dt and splits when max(physW,physH) > target (lines 332-338) â€” but the split is ALWAYS the 4-way square split (split(), lines 530-540), so local anisotropy cannot change cell shape; only the global 2^B:1 root does. Making it metric-driven entails: per-node (hU, hT) targets â€” directional normal curvature along u and along t instead of principalCurvatureMax â€” plus F (full 2x2 metric/eigenpair) for shear; a per-axis Lipschitz grading; and shouldRefine returning WHICH axis to split. That last part is where the 2:1 balance constrains hard: balance() is keyed on the single square 'level' (PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:406-439), and the transition templates support exactly ONE mid-vertex per side ('the only options are same, one-coarser, or one-finer' â€” QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:283-287). The two existing escape hatches show the cost: (a) GLOBAL uBias is free because eUL=level+B is a bijection of level (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:209-211, 237-238) â€” all balance machinery is unchanged; (b) per-leaf u-only uExtra needed its OWN eUL 2:1 balance pass (balanceEffectiveU, PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:891-916), is capped at MAX_U_EXTRA=4 (line 95), excluded from square balance (line 413), boundary-exempt, and its both-axis balance cascade caused the documented ArtDeco build-timeout (WatertightAssembly.ts:382-387). There is NO t-extra analogue; a true per-axis refinement needs a second directional mechanism plus a 2-axis balance â€” a structural extension, not a parameter.

Q4 â€” Yes, the relief-gated uBias is GLOBAL and that is the sliver mechanism. B multiplies u-columns everywhere: root 2^B x 1 (PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:347-350); square splits preserve 2^B:1 u:t at every level (doc lines 159-167); one B per wall, computed once from the OUTER sampler and shared by both walls so the rings match (WatertightAssembly.ts:348-374; ConformingWall.ts:496-497 even re-pins the boundary coarser by B to keep nRing). Two over-correction channels, both in-code: (1) locally-isotropic/t-dominant bands â€” FShearDiagnostics.ts:108-111 documents maxTRatio as exactly this lever: 'uBias B MULTIPLIES this band's 3D aspect by 2^B, so it bounds how much bias is safe (the GAP-1 over-correction lever)'; a band with sqrt(G)>=sqrt(E) becomes a 2^B:1 t-long sliver. Per-cell/per-region mechanisms: only the uExtra directional u-refine exists (localDirectionalRefine, PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:845-883), and it can only NARROW cells in u â€” it cannot locally undo a global B for t-dominant bands; it is default-OFF (WatertightAssembly.ts:393-396), hard-gated to wide/flat dims (directionalGateTripped, lines 826-842, 853-854), disabled on feature walls (ConformingWall.ts:445), and capped at uExtra=4. The crease biasFreeU mechanism restores t-DEPTH on crease cells but only by deepening square splits â€” it is not a local un-bias. A per-cell relief-scaled B was tried earlier and measured harmful (WatertightAssembly.ts:40-52). So today uBias is effectively all-or-nothing per wall.

Q5 â€” creaseRefine/biasFreeU and the watertight template. Producer: buildCreaseRefineLines (FeatureLineGraph.ts:1030-1126) â€” for each vertical-crease LOCUS it resolves the PRE-warp source column (warp anchor whose target~locus, else the locus itself for dyadic/seam columns the warp drops â€” lines 1040-1046; this fixed GeometricStar/GothicArches residuals, doc 1004-1013), helix base anchors (1065-1073), and horizontal-band SOURCE rows via the t-warp (sourceForBand 1081-1087; boundary-pinned rows skipped 1093-1098). Consumption: ConformingWall.ts:529-532 builds the intersector ONLY when creaseLines exist AND uBias>0; threaded through buildQuadtreeAtScale (198, 214) into the quadtree, where the onCrease gate (PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:376-379: creaseRefine && uBiasLevel>0 && intersects) flips biasFreeU in shouldRefine (line 384). shouldRefine (320-339) then uses the BIAS-FREE u-width wTestSize = 1/2^level instead of 1/2^(level+B) (line 334) â€” ONLY the refinement DECISION ignores the bias; cell geometry stays biased (doc 314-318); perfect no-op at B=0. THE WATERTIGHT TEMPLATE (the interior-independent, edge-local shared-point pattern any new fix must follow): the mechanism never invents or moves a vertex â€” it only changes WHICH dyadic leaves exist; conformity then falls out of four invariants: (1) every vertex is a dyadic grid-line point (corner or side midpoint) deduped by quantized key â€” vertexIndex, QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:253-271, QSCALE=1<<24 exact for dyadic coords (line 51), seam closed by index remap; (2) a side's mid-vertex existence is decided by an edge-LOCAL probe of the shared leaf set (sideHasFiner 288-317, uColHasFiner 240-251) â€” both neighbours derive the identical answer from the same edge data, never from either cell's interior; (3) 2:1 balance runs AFTER all refine decisions (constructor order: refine -> enforcePinnedBoundary -> balance, PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:205-208) so crease-deepened cells get legal transitions automatically, and levelCap (239-247) keeps the pinned rings at nRing; (4) where a choice IS made inside a cell it must be interior-only â€” exactly the contract the dead earClipMaxMinAngle states: 'Pure interior choice: the polygon vertices are unchanged, so every shared edge keeps its exact vertex set (watertight + T-junction-free preserved)' (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:138-148), which is why the handoff's efg-activation fix is watertight-safe by the same argument.
